title: Shekha Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Shekha Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Shekha Bird Sanctuary is a bird sanctuary at a 25 ha (62 acres) lake near the village of Shekha, 17 km (11 mi) east of Aligarh and 5 km (3.1 mi) from the Grand Trunk Road (GT Road)
overview.description: Shekha Bird Sanctuary is a bird sanctuary at a 25 ha (62 acres) lake near the village of Shekha, 17 km (11 mi) east of Aligarh and 5 km (3.1 mi) from the Grand Trunk Road (GT Road) in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. It is notable for birding as many birds overwinter there. It is a fresh water perennial water body that came into existence after the formation of the Upper Ganges Canal in 1852 which flows adjacent to the lake. It is maintained by the Forest Department.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shekha Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shekha Bird Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shekha Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (82 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (81 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shekha Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shekha Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shekha Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Shekha Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shekha Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pilkhana, Uttar Pradesh, Jalali, Uttar Pradesh, Kauriaganj.
